recent_trades.summary: Adding to EOG on +0.33 sentiment and analyst notes it is poised for a surge, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: EOG holds near 30D highs with positive dividend-growth coverage and long-term ownership signals from Capital World; lifting from 8% to 10% is a modest sizing that aligns with energy-sector resilience and uses proceeds from trimming weaker tech.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing PM to 13% on top sentiment reading of +0.43 and consistent uptrend, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: PM displays steady gains, defensive high-yield characteristics, and positive sustainability headlines; raising from ~10% stays well under 20% cap, improves portfolio balance, and uses capital from the paired sales.

recent_trades.summary: Further trimming ADBE after 3.8% drop and persistent red candles, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ADBE remains the weakest chart in the tech basket, well off 30D highs with only neutral sentiment and no positive catalysts; reducing from 6% to 3% releases capital while staying within the prior partial-sell logic.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting AVGO entirely on Jefferies downgrade, neutral sentiment, and failure to hold recent highs, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Technicals show stall after 30D range test; news explicitly notes Broadcom cut from top picks while Amazon and McDonald’s added. Full exit frees 1.5% of portfolio for higher-conviction names and respects the partial trim taken earlier today.

recent_trades.summary: Opening a 2% position in Intel on strong 6% 5-day momentum, positive sentiment, and attractive valuation near 30-day highs.
recent_trades.reasoning: Intel has rallied 6.18% in 5 days and 4.78% in 30 days with sentiment at +0.26. The stock is at its 30-day high, indicating continued momentum. We have no current position. A 2% allocation is prudent for a new entry, funded from cash.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Starbucks to a 4% weight based on positive trend momentum and supportive analyst sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: Starbucks is generating positive news coverage (+0.21 sentiment) noting its continued attractiveness despite a strong YTD run. We are increasing our allocation from 2.6% to 4% to ride the current momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Intel to reach a 6% weight to capitalize on its strong technical momentum and favorable growth narratives.
recent_trades.reasoning: Intel demonstrates robust technical strength with a +6.18% gain over the last 5 days. Coupled with positive sentiment as a growth and dividend play, we are adding to our position to maximize exposure to its ongoing rally.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Amazon to a 9% weight following bullish analyst calls predicting a potential Magnificent Seven breakout.
recent_trades.reasoning: Amazon is seeing strong positive sentiment (+0.24) and has been named as a top pick and potential leader for the next tech breakout. We are increasing our weight from 7% to 9% to capture this expected upside.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Exxon Mobil completely due to negative sentiment and overvaluation concerns to reallocate capital.
recent_trades.reasoning: Sentiment for Exxon Mobil has turned negative (-0.11), with recent reports highlighting the stock as overvalued. Exiting this position entirely frees up approximately 5% of the portfolio to be deployed into higher-conviction opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 5% position in INTC — strongest 5D momentum in universe at +6.18%, breaking out to 30D highs with positive sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC at $112.04 is at 30D high of $112.28, massive breakout from $102.40 low. +4.8% recent gain on chart. Positive sentiment at +0.26. High volume stock with strong momentum. 'Outstanding growth stock' headline supportive.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting remaining MSFT — persistent downtrend from $432 to $419, already trimmed twice today, near 30D lows with rising yield headwinds.
recent_trades.reasoning: Already trimmed from 10% to 5% to 3% today. The trend is clearly down (-2.4% on 5D). Rising 30Y yields near 5.14% are particularly negative for high-multiple software. Better to fully exit and potentially re-enter at better levels.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting AVGO — Jefferies cut from top picks, negative unrealized P&L at -1.5%, neutral sentiment with no clear catalyst.
recent_trades.reasoning: Headline says Broadcom gets cut from Jefferies top picks. Trading below avg cost at -1.50% unrealized. Neutral sentiment. Better to redeploy this 4% into stronger names.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Exxon slightly as Chevron is showing better relative strength and Exxon’s sentiment has turned mildly negative, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: XOM is near the top of its range, but relative strength appears inferior to CVX and the stock-specific news tone is slightly negative. Reducing from 6.81% to 5.00% funds stronger opportunities within energy while still maintaining exposure to the sector trend.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to Chevron as energy strength persists and the stock is breaking toward the top of its recent range, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: CVX is sitting essentially at its 30-day high with constructive intraday price action and slightly better sentiment than XOM. The portfolio already has energy exposure, so this is only a moderate increase from 4.03% to 6.00%, favoring the stronger integrated name without overconcentrating the sector.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Nvidia as it presses a fresh 30-day high with the strongest AI-linked sentiment in the book, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA is trading near its 30-day high after a positive intraday push, and the chart shows continued higher highs unlike the weaker software cohort. News flow remains favorable around AI leadership and customer demand, so increasing from 6.10% to 8.00% keeps sizing disciplined while leaning into one of the clearest momentum leaders.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to AMZN on positive Jefferies top pick and Magnificent Seven breakout headlines, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Current 2% weight is underweight versus portfolio peers; +0.24 sentiment and 'could lead next breakout' narrative not fully reflected in modest -1% move. Sizing doubles exposure modestly without exceeding 20% cap.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming ADBE on sharp -4.0% intraday drop and continued downtrend in chart, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Despite +0.11 sentiment and software rebound mentions, technicals show sustained weakness below SMA with elevated weight over 8%; partial trim to 6% reduces risk while keeping some exposure to positive AI narrative.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating INTC position on +3.2% intraday surge, +0.26 sentiment and growth stock buy recommendation, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Chart shows strong green candles and breakout above recent range; positive headlines position it as a no-brainer buy on any dip with AI tailwinds. New 5% target fits within concentration limits and diversifies tech exposure without repeating recent trades.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to NVDA from 8% to 10% — hitting 30D highs at $223.37 with strong positive sentiment and market power narrative.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA is at its 30D high with +1.1% today, positive sentiment at +0.38, and headlines about holding unprecedented market power alongside AAPL. The breakout above the 30D range with volume support justifies adding. Strong AI demand thesis intact.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Chevron from 4% to 2% in response to reports that Berkshire Hathaway has reduced its stake in the company.
recent_trades.reasoning: Berkshire Hathaway's reduction of its Chevron position introduces negative sentiment and a potential overhang on the stock. Trimming the position to 2% manages risk while freeing up cash to deploy into higher-conviction AI infrastructure names.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 3% position in Oracle following news that it is among the first to receive Nvidia's next-generation Vera chips, reinforcing its AI cloud positioning.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle's involvement in Nvidia's newest chip rollout highlights its growing importance in the AI infrastructure space. Despite a recent 30-day pullback of 1.71%, this fundamental catalyst provides a solid entry point for long-term growth and diversifies our tech holdings.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Visa as payments sentiment is good but the stock remains weak at the bottom of its recent range, confidence 7/10.

While headlines are mildly positive, MA appears to have the stronger thematic setup, so removing the weaker payments exposure is the cleaner choice.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the remaining Amazon stub as the stock still lags near its 30-day low despite positive headlines, confidence 8/10.

I have already reduced it multiple times today; fully exiting the residual 1% position simplifies the book and frees capital for higher-momentum setups.

recent_trades.summary: Starting a modest Intel position as it breaks toward a 30-day high with improving momentum and supportive sentiment, confidence 7/10.

News flow is mildly positive and the chart shows a steady intraday climb, so a starter allocation is warranted, but I am keeping sizing moderate because semiconductor exposure is already meaningful through NVDA.

recent_trades.reasoning: META has underperformed recently with -2.29% unrealized loss and AI-related restructuring news creating near-term pressure; frees capital for higher-conviction names like NVDA/INTC; avoids redundancy as not traded today.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing NVDA toward 5% on +0.38 sentiment, repeated headlines as AI market power, and resilient price action near highs, confidence 8/10.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting ADBE as the stock is down -3.8% today, sitting at cost basis with no gains, and the recovery thesis has not materialized.

While sentiment today is mildly positive (+0.11) from software rebound narrative, the technical picture shows a clear downtrend approaching 30D lows. The position is small (2.01%) and the capital can be better deployed. Cutting losses here before further deterioration.

recent_trades.summary: Further trimming MSFT to ~3% — continued weakness at -2.8% today, now below $420 and at 30D lows despite positive sentiment, confidence 6/10.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting ADBE — hitting 30D lows at -3.8% today, persistent downtrend from $265 to $254 with no reversal signal, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ADBE is at its 30-day low of $254.30, down nearly 4% today. The chart shows a clear multi-day downtrend with no support forming. While sentiment is slightly positive on software rebound news, the price action is contradicting it. Better to redeploy this capital elsewhere.

recent_trades.reasoning: Qualcomm is up 2.36% over the last 5 days with positive market sentiment and solid technicals. We are allocating a small position to diversify our semiconductor exposure and participate in its steady upward momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 4% position in Intel to capitalize on its strong recent momentum and positive growth narratives.
recent_trades.reasoning: Intel is exhibiting significant relative strength, up over 6% in the last 5 days. Supported by highly positive sentiment (+0.26) and inclusion in prominent growth stock lists, the technical breakout appears legitimate and warrants a new long position.

recent_trades.summary: Selling Walmart to free up capital following negative sentiment and the loss of a key supply chain executive to Target.
recent_trades.reasoning: Walmart is showing negative sentiment (-0.19) and underperforming the broader market slightly. The departure of a top supply chain executive to a direct competitor introduces operational risk, making this a prudent time to harvest capital for better opportunities.

recent_trades.reasoning: Earlier today, we trimmed BLK to manage risk. With headlines now confirming that the 30-year Treasury yield is nearing 5.14% and the bond selloff is deepening, the macro environment is increasingly hostile for asset management multiples. A full exit protects capital.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to ConocoPhillips from 2.02% to 3.0% on very positive broker sentiment (+0.54) and oil price support.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Disney from 1.98% to 3.0% on very positive sentiment (+0.39) and potential brand strength catalyst.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Visa as payments sentiment is constructive but the stock is still pinned at the bottom of its recent range, confidence 6/10.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Amazon further as it continues to lag near its 30-day low despite favorable headlines, confidence 7/10.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming MSFT from ~7% to 5% — persistent downtrend from $432 to $419, near 30D lows with rising yield headwinds.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Nvidia to reach a 6% weight to capitalize on its continued market dominance and AI momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: Nvidia continues to benefit from extremely positive sentiment (+0.38) and is cited as holding unprecedented structural market power alongside Apple. I am increasing the position from 4% to 6% to ensure sufficient exposure to this secular growth trend.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Amazon to a 7% portfolio weight after being named a top pick by Jefferies.
recent_trades.reasoning: Amazon has been designated a top pick by Jefferies and is highlighted as a potential leader for the next Magnificent Seven breakout. The stock offers a favorable risk/reward profile here to increase exposure, supported by strong positive news sentiment (+0.24).

recent_trades.reasoning: Oppenheimer recently stated that Costco is seen missing quarterly earnings views, introducing near-term fundamental risk. Exiting this 5.2% position protects the portfolio from potential downside surprises and frees up cash for higher-conviction growth opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Apple from 4.01% to 3.5% as it holds near 30-day lows with mixed sentiment, taking profit on a +5.65% gain.
recent_trades.reasoning: AAPL's recent price action is flat with slightly negative 5D performance, but it still has a solid gain. Reducing weight to 3.5% rebalances the portfolio and raises cash for potential better entries.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming NVIDIA from 4.02% to 3.5% to lock in gains after strong positive sentiment and reduce single-name concentration.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA has been a strong performer with +7.20% unrealized gain and high positive sentiment. Its weight is near the 4% level, and trimming slightly reduces concentration without missing upside, freeing cash for potential dips.

recent_trades.summary: Starting a modest COP position as strong sentiment and resilient energy leadership support a low-risk entry, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: COP has the strongest news sentiment in the energy group and is holding a constructive range close to its recent highs while the broader energy complex remains one of the firmer areas of the market. Position size stays modest because it is a new name and not clearly breaking out, but it adds diversification within a sector that is outperforming growth today.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AMZN again as it remains near the bottom of its 30-day range despite positive headlines, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN is still one of the weakest names in the portfolio on both 5-day and 30-day performance and sits just above its 30-day low, showing poor relative strength versus other large-cap growth holdings. News sentiment is positive, but that improvement has not translated into price confirmation, so a further modest trim frees capital from a laggard without fully exiting in case momentum turns later today.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating GE position on strong +0.25 sentiment, AI advancements in aerospace, and positive engine contract news, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: No current holding; technicals show resilience near 284 support with green candle close. News on AI applications and China plane engines not fully priced in per 30D performance, sized to 5% to stay diversified.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

mvp_trade.summary: Taking full profits on CSCO after a 14.58% gain, as sentiment turns negative despite strong earnings.

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P -0.17%, Nasdaq -0.27%, Dow -0.20%. Real Estate led sectors (+1.22%) while Materials lagged (-1.38%). Gemini 3.1 Pro led the day at +0.65%. 3 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek Reasoner at +22.32%. Key headline: The Fed will have to raise interest rates in July to appease 'bond vigilantes,' Yardeni says - CNBC — broadly positive for equities.
